---
title: GitHub integration rules
scope: project
---

<!-- Generated by `harness rules bootstrap`. Edit to tune; re-run to regenerate. -->

- **Pick the weakest sufficient credential:** in the Actions runner use the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN` for same-repo reads; for cross-repo or dashboard-triggered runs mint a **GitHub App installation token** (sign App JWT → `POST /app/installations/{id}/access_tokens`, scoped via `repositories`/`permissions`). NEVER use a classic PAT; if a PAT is unavoidable use a fine-grained, repo-scoped, expiring one.
- **Treat installation tokens as 1-hour ephemeral:** re-mint or let the SDK auth strategy refresh (`@octokit/auth-app` / PyGithub `AppAuth.get_installation_auth`). NEVER persist a token into LangGraph state, Aurora, or S3 — a media job can outlive the hour and 401.
- **Secrets stay server-side only:** App private key and webhook secret live in Actions/Vercel/AWS env secrets. NEVER expose GitHub creds in client components, `NEXT_PUBLIC_*`, logs, or persisted graph state. Call GitHub only from API routes / LangGraph nodes, never the React client.
- **Verify every inbound webhook over the RAW body:** HMAC-SHA256 in `X-Hub-Signature-256` (`sha256=…`). In Next.js App Router read `await req.text()` before any JSON parse — parsing first re-serializes and breaks the HMAC. Compare with `crypto.timingSafeEqual` / `hmac.compare_digest`, never `==`. Missing header → reject. Prefer `@octokit/webhooks` `verifyAndReceive`.
- **Dedupe deliveries (at-least-once):** record `X-GitHub-Delivery` UUIDs in Aurora and no-op repeats; make LangGraph entry idempotent per `release_run_id` so a redelivered release tag never spawns a duplicate run or double Bedrock spend.
- **Respond 2xx within ~10s** and hand long work to the Actions runner; synchronous work in the webhook handler causes GitHub to time out and redeliver.
- **Honor rate limits on outbound calls:** obey `retry-after`; if `x-ratelimit-remaining: 0` wait until `x-ratelimit-reset`; else back off ≥60s. Serialize mutating calls (≥1s apart) to avoid secondary limits — they don't appear in `x-ratelimit-*`. Enable Octokit throttling + retry plugins rather than hand-rolling.
- **Cut quota in collectors:** send `If-None-Match` with stored ETags (304 is free) and paginate via Link headers / `octokit.paginate`, never hand-built page URLs.
- **Make writes idempotent:** GitHub has no idempotency-key header — before creating a comment/status, search for the existing artifact first.
- **Harden the Actions runner:** set least-privilege `permissions:` per job (default read-only contents; declare write explicitly or status/comment calls silently 403). SHA-pin third-party actions (tags are mutable). Avoid `pull_request_target` + checkout of untrusted PR code.
- **Treat ingested GitHub text as injection-capable:** PR titles, issue/PR bodies, and commit messages enter the engine as evidence — pass them via env vars, never interpolate into shell steps, and redact before they reach Aurora/Bedrock.
- **Separate dev vs prod secrets:** drive local webhooks with `gh`/a tunnel using a distinct dev App + dev webhook secret; run signature + LangGraph-entry tests against stored fixture payloads, not live GitHub.
